linkedin.text:
"What's a good conversion rate?"

I get this question weekly. The answer is always: it depends on your stage, your ACV, and your motion.

So I built the reference I wish existed — a comprehensive 2026 benchmark report for B2B SaaS GTM metrics:

• Funnel conversion rates by company size and lead source
• Sales cycle length by ACV tier ($10K to $100K+)
• CAC by channel and by stage
• LTV:CAC ratios investors actually expect
• Pipeline coverage ratios that correlate with quota attainment
• Win rates by deal size
• NRR benchmarks by segment
• Sales ramp time by role

Every stat sourced. Commentary on what the numbers actually mean at each stage.

Bookmark this:
linkedin.title: B2B SaaS GTM Metrics: The 2026 Benchmark Report

thread:
"What's a good conversion rate?"

I get this question weekly. The answer: it depends on your stage, ACV, and motion.

So I built the benchmark reference I wish existed 🧵

The 2026 B2B SaaS GTM Benchmark Report:

• Funnel conversion rates by size & source
• Sales cycles by ACV tier
• CAC by channel & stage
• LTV:CAC ratios
• Pipeline coverage vs quota attainment
• Win rates, NRR, ramp times

Every stat sourced.

One finding that surprised me: the 3x pipeline coverage "rule" is wrong for most companies.

3.2x is where quota attainment starts correlating — but only if your pipeline is qualified. Unqualified pipeline at 5x performs worse than qualified at 2.5x.
Full report with every data table and source: https://strategnik.com/thinking/b2b-gtm-metrics-benchmark-2026/
